mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate transmits tighter discounting and higher equity hurdle rates, so higher policy rates predict lower S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is already elevated, because real-rate pressure and recession risk rise together.
failure_condition: Reject if higher FEDFUNDS does not coincide with lower 20d forward SPX returns in triggered samples, or if the sign of the effect turns positive in a rolling 3-year window with stable inflation above trend.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening labor income and consumer demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the conditional Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 4-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad household and business demand strength, improving revenue expectations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST strength fails to outperform baseline on 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in any expanding 3-year estimation window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in INDPRO signals softening industrial output and weakening cyclical earnings momentum, which should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the fall is abrupt rather than gradual.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by at least 0.5% over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if sharp INDPRO declines are not followed by below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign becomes positive in consecutive manufacturing-slowdown episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real policy is loose and liquidity conditions favor equity multiples. This combination predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes do not produce higher-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the joint condition loses predictive power after policy-rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A broad contraction in GDPC1 following a softening in PAYEMS indicates recessionary demand loss, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarter and PAYEMS growth turns negative on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-and-payroll contraction episodes fail to lead to negative 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal is weaker than either series alone over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A low or falling UNRATE in a mature expansion supports household income stability and reduces recession pricing, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is flat or falls over the latest 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if stable-or-falling unemployment does not improve 5d forward SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the effect flips negative when unemployment is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in INDPRO and PAYEMS identifies improving production and hiring momentum, which should lift cyclically sensitive earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint industrial and payroll improvement does not outperform baseline on 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.
